anti_patterns.name: c_style_casts
anti_patterns.description: C-style casts bypass type safety. Use static_cast, dynamic_cast, const_cast, or reinterpret_cast.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect (Type)expr patterns that aren't function calls.
anti_patterns.fix: Replace (int)x with static_cast<int>(x). Use dynamic_cast for polymorphic types.

anti_patterns.name: using_namespace_std
anti_patterns.description: Avoid 'using namespace std' in headers. It pollutes the global namespace and causes name collisions.
anti_patterns.fix: Use explicit std:: prefix or selective using declarations (using std::vector;) in .cpp files only.

anti_patterns.name: raw_c_arrays
anti_patterns.description: Use std::array for fixed-size arrays and std::vector for dynamic arrays instead of raw C arrays.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect C-style array declarations: int arr[10].
anti_patterns.fix: Replace int arr[10] with std::array<int, 10> arr. Replace dynamic arrays with std::vector.

anti_patterns.name: raw_c_strings
anti_patterns.description: Prefer std::string and std::string_view over const char* for string handling.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect const char* parameter or variable declarations used for string data.
anti_patterns.fix: Use std::string for owned strings and std::string_view for read-only references.

anti_patterns.name: missing_noexcept
anti_patterns.description: Move constructors and move assignment operators should be marked noexcept for optimal container performance.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect move constructor/assignment without noexcept specifier.
anti_patterns.fix: Add noexcept to move operations: T(T&&) noexcept; T& operator=(T&&) noexcept;

anti_patterns.name: endl_overuse
anti_patterns.description: std::endl flushes the buffer every time. Use '\n' for newlines unless you specifically need a flush.
anti_patterns.fix: Replace std::endl with '\n'. Use std::endl only when you explicitly need to flush.
